Text: 1. What component acts like a "kink in a hose" to limit the flow of electricity?
Rubric: Award 8 points for identifying "Resistor".

Text: 2. The battery pack in your Smart Garden supplies DC power. What does DC stand for?
Rubric: Award 8 points for identifying "Direct Current".

Text: 3. What unit of measurement is used to measure electrical pressure (Voltage)?
Rubric: Award 8 points for identifying "Volts" or "V".

Text: 4. What happens if you install an LED into your circuit backwards?
Rubric: Award 8 points for stating it will not turn on or light up because LEDs only allow current in one direction.

Text: 5. Write the standard mathematical formula for Ohm's Law.
Rubric: Award 8 points for V = I * R (or V = IR).

Text: 6. Which circuit symbol is drawn as a plain rectangle?
Rubric: Award 4 points for Resistor (index 2).

Text: 7. What do the colored bands painted on a fixed resistor tell you?
Option_C: Which way to plug it in
Option_D: If it uses AC or DC power
Rubric: Award 4 points for "Its resistance value and tolerance".

Text: 8. To measure voltage the multimeter probes must be connected:
Option_C: Only when the power is OFF
Rubric: Award 4 points for "In parallel (across the component)".

Text: 9. Which component acts as the main power source for the Smart Garden?
Rubric: Award 4 points for "The 7.4V Battery Pack".

Text: 10. What is the function of a switch in a circuit?
Option_C: To break or complete the path of electricity
Option_D: To change colors of the LED
Rubric: Award 4 points for "To break or complete the path of electricity".

Text: 1. Your plant monitoring system is powered by a 7.4V battery pack. Total resistance is 330 ohms. Calculate the exact current flowing through the system.
Rubric: Award 4 points for formula (I=V/R) and values. Award 4 points for answer: 0.022A or 22mA.

Text: 2. You find a resistor painted Brown, Black, Red, Gold. Calculate its nominal value and state the acceptable minimum and maximum resistance based on tolerance.
Rubric: Award 4 pts for nominal value (1000 ohms / 1k ohm). Award 4 pts for tolerance range: 950 ohms to 1050 ohms.

Text: 3. A student reads 0.00A on a circuit that is switched ON. Write a checklist of three physical connections they should check.
Rubric: Award 8 pts for 3 valid physical checks (LED backwards / battery disconnected / switch seated wrong / meter not in series). 4 pts for 1-2 checks.

Text: 4. Match each scenario to Voltage, Current, or Resistance: a) Water pump blocked by debris. b) Connecting multimeter without breaking circuit. c) Removing component completely to test safely.
Rubric: Award 8 pts total. a) Resistance (3 pts). b) Voltage (3 pts). c) Resistance (2 pts).

Text: 5. Explain using the water hose analogy why adding a second resistor in series makes the LED dimmer.
Rubric: Award 8 points for explaining a resistor is a kink in the hose and a second kink further restricts flow (current) leaving less to power the LED.

Text: 6. The switch is closed but the LED does not turn on. Most likely cause?
Option_A: The LED long leg (anode) is connected to 0V.
Rubric: Award 4 points for "The LED long leg (anode) is connected to 0V."

Text: 7. To measure voltage across a single resistor in a functioning circuit you must:
Option_A: Connect probes across the resistor while power is ON.
Option_B: Break circuit and insert in series.
Option_C: Turn power OFF and remove resistor.
Rubric: Award 4 points for "Connect probes across the resistor while power is ON."

Text: 8. A design calls for 150 ohms but you only have standard E12 values. Which component do you select?
Rubric: Award 4 points for 150 ohms (it IS a standard E12 value).

Text: 9. Why do we strictly use DC power in the garden projects instead of AC?
Option_A: DC flows in one direction and is a safe voltage.
Option_B: AC does not work with resistors.
Option_C: DC reverses direction safer for plants.
Rubric: Award 4 points for "DC flows in one direction and is a safe voltage."

Text: 10. You want to adjust light sensitivity daily without tools. Which component is correct?
Rubric: Award 4 points for "Potentiometer (Variable Resistor)".

Text: 1. You need an LED to drop 2.0V from a 7.4V battery pack. Current must be 20mA (0.020A). Calculate the exact resistance needed then state the E12 resistor to use.
Rubric: Award 4 pts for voltage drop (5.4V). Award 2 pts for ideal resistance (270 ohms). Award 2 pts for noting 270 ohms IS an E12 value.

Text: 2. You measure a Red-Red-Brown-Gold resistor. The meter reads 215 ohms. Prove mathematically if it is safe to use.
Rubric: Award 4 pts for nominal value (220 ohms +/- 5%). Award 4 pts for calculating range (209 to 231 ohms) and concluding 215 ohms is safe.

Text: 3. Draw or describe the exact probe placement to measure current flowing through the LED. What must be done to the physical wires?
Rubric: Award 4 pts for stating the circuit must be broken. Award 4 pts for describing the multimeter in SERIES (flow goes into one probe and out the other).

Text: 4. Match each incorrect color code translation to the mistake made: a) "Brown-Black-Red" read as 102 ohms. b) "Red-Red-Brown" read as 2200 ohms. c) 470 ohm resistor read backwards as Brown-Violet-Yellow.
Rubric: Award 8 pts total. a) Band 3 read as a digit instead of a multiplier (3 pts). b) Multiplied by 100 instead of 10 (3 pts). c) Read from the tolerance end (2 pts).

Text: 5. A classmate sets the meter to ohms and probes a resistor while the 7.4V battery is still ON. Describe two consequences.
Rubric: Award 4 pts for stating the reading will be inaccurate or false. Award 4 pts for stating it will likely blow the meter fuse.

Text: 6. A circuit requires 315 ohms. You use an E12 330 ohm resistor. How will this affect the current?
Rubric: Award 4 points for "Current will decrease slightly."

Text: 7. You measure 7.4V across an OPEN switch and 0V when the switch is CLOSED. This means:
Rubric: Award 4 points for "Normal electrical behavior."

Text: 8. A student's LED flashes briefly then permanently dies. Which resistor color code was most likely used?
Rubric: Award 4 points for "Brown-Black-Black-Gold (10 ohms)" - too low a resistance caused overcurrent.

Text: 9. If the multiplier band (Band 3) is Orange the resistor value is in the:
Rubric: Award 4 points for "Thousands of Ohms (kOhm)".

Text: 10. In a series circuit if you swap the position of the resistor and the LED:
Rubric: Award 4 points for "Circuit functions exactly the same."

Text: A student receives a failing grade because their 12mA soil moisture sensor burned out. They powered it with 7.4V using a Brown-Black-Brown-Gold resistor. Prove mathematically why it failed.
Rubric: Decoding resistor: 100 ohms. Current: I = 7.4 / 100 = 74mA. Conclude 74mA destroys a 12mA sensor. Full marks for all three steps.

Text: Reverse-engineer: you need to drop 5V across a resistor at exactly 10mA. Calculate the required resistance select the best E12 value and write the exact four color bands.
Rubric: R = V/I = 5 / 0.010 = 500 ohms. Nearest E12 value: 470 ohms. Color bands: Yellow Violet Brown Gold. Full marks for all three steps.

Text: A fully charged 8.4V battery powers a 2.0V LED. Calculate the minimum E12 resistor value to ensure the current NEVER exceeds 25mA.
Rubric: Voltage drop: 8.4 - 2.0 = 6.4V. Resistance: 6.4 / 0.025 = 256 ohms. Nearest HIGHER E12 value (for safety): 270 ohms. Full marks for all three steps.
